# FUNCTIONING_CLIENT.md — the decompiled RSC client boots, logs in, and renders the live world

This is the honest achievement summary for the deobfuscated rev ~233–235 J++ `rsclassic`
client (decompiled in `../src/`). It now **compiles, boots, autologins against a live
local OpenRSC server, holds a stable multi-minute session, and renders the live in-game
3D world.** Reproduce it with [RUN.md](RUN.md).

> Scope note: the deob was originally optimized for *readability/correctness as a
> comprehension reference*, intentionally not for recompilation. Getting it to actually
> **run** was a second phase: it surfaced a class of runtime bugs that a static read can't
> — and fixing them validated the deob against real server traffic and a real renderer.

---

## The milestone ladder (all reached)

| Milestone | What it proves | State |
|---|---|---|
| **M0 — tree compiles** | The whole client compiles to bytecode under JDK 17. | ✅ **0 errors / 71 classes** |
| **M1 — boots + opens window** | `Boot` → `Mudclient.main` → `GameShell.startApplication` → AWT frame + loader thread, logo + runtime-rasterized fonts. | ✅ |
| **M2 — title screen + content** | `loadGameConfig` fetches all 12 content packs + CRC manifest over HTTP (CacheUpdater fills `_junkArray`), draws the login/title screen. | ✅ |
| **M3 — connects + logs in** | RSA-encrypted login block, register-then-login over 43594, in-protocol handshake against the live OpenRSC server. | ✅ (autologin `deobtest`) |
| **M4 — live world frame** | A real in-game frame: terrain, walls, scenery, lighting from live region data. | ✅ |
| **M5 — stable session** | Session held for **2–4+ minutes** with no disconnect; per-tick coord updates, region tracking, all wired UI tabs render crash-free. | ✅ |
| **+ live 3D** | The 3D viewport renders the live world at **93–94% non-black**, with player movement (route + walk packets + op-191 updates) and the server "Welcome"/chat overlays drawing real data. | ✅ |

> Note on ladder ordering: M4 (a renderable world frame) was actually reachable just
> before the full live login (M3) handshake was wired — the world-frame and the
> login/session paths were brought up in close succession, then both stabilized into the
> stable session (M5).

| File | What it shows |

| `/tmp/rsc-run/play-walked.png` | Clean framebuffer dump (no AWT) of the stone-walled interior post-walk: stone walls with torch brackets, wooden-plank + checkered floor, the chat-tab bar ("All messages / Chat history / Quest history / Private history") across the top. The lighter overlaid rectangle on the right with a sharp seam is the known floor-shading fidelity quirk (below), **not** a crash. |

| `/tmp/rsc-run/live3d.png`, `/tmp/rsc-run/fbuffer.png` | Latest framebuffer dumps — `fbuffer.png` with the Welcome box up, `live3d.png` with it dismissed for a clean viewport. |

---

## The class of runtime bugs found-by-running

These are the **kinds** of defect a static read cannot catch but a running client + live
server immediately expose. Each was fixed against the clean decompile
(`reference/rsc-client/decompiled/normalized-clean/client.java` et al.) as ground truth —
**nothing invented**. Representative examples from this client:

- **Split obfuscated fields (one logical field deobfuscated into two).** The biggest
  category. The deob occasionally split a single obf field into two readable names; the
  *initializer/allocation* landed on one alias while *all reads* used the other, so a
  field that looked correct was silently always-null/always-zero at runtime.
  - *Example — walk path arrays:* `walkPathX`/`walkPathY` (read in `walkTo`) and the
    `new int[8000]` allocations (`Rg`/`pf`) were the same logical field split in two; the
    allocation hit the orphans, so `World.route` got `null` arrays → NPE. Fix: repoint the
    allocations at `walkPathX`/`walkPathY`, drop the orphans. (Clean `client.java`
    allocates exactly one pair and routes through it.)
  - *Example — region origin:* `walkTo` built the absolute walk-start from
    `regionX`/`regionY`, which were never-assigned orphan dups (always 0); the real region
    origin is `Qg`/`zg` (set in `loadRegion`). Every walk packet carried a start tile
    ~144 tiles off, so the **server silently dropped it and the player never moved**. Fix:
    `regionX→Qg`, `regionY→zg` at all 4 sites. (Clean `client.java` emits `Qg + var2` /
    `zg + var1`.)
- **Dropped/relocated static initializers.** Obfuscated static-init blocks whose ordering
  or presence the deob disturbed — a constant/table that reads correct in source but is
  uninitialized at the moment of first use.
- **Inverted operators / flattened boolean idioms.** Obfuscation flattens `x != 0`,
  `~x != -1`, sign tricks, and bit-inversions; a one-character sense error
  (`!=` vs `==`, a missing `~`) compiles fine and only manifests as wrong control flow at
  runtime (e.g. a `loginScreenMode != 0` gate).
- **Live stubs surfacing as gaps.** Methods that were summarized/aliased during the read
  pass but turn out to be on a hot live path — e.g. the **stats/skills tab (`qc==3`)**:
  its drawer (clean obf `c(boolean,int)`) is absent from this deob's Mudclient with no
  readable alias, so that one tab is disabled (flagged in-code at its dispatch).
- **Encrypted-opcode dispatch.** Inbound/outbound packets use ISAAC-keyed opcode
  ciphering; an off-by-one or wrong-handler in the dispatch table only shows up against a
  real server's traffic (validated here against OpenRSC Payload235 opcodes).

### Investigated and confirmed NOT a bug

- **"error in sprite clipping routine" flood.** This is the **authentic vanilla RSC
  diagnostic** (clean `ua.java`, swallowed by `catch(Exception)`). The four scaled-sprite
  clip routines + `setBounds`/`resetBounds` were verified faithful to the clean decompile.
  The flood was transient/data-dependent and did not recur in later runs. Left in place
  unchanged.

## Commit trail

The runtime bring-up lives in this run of commits (most recent last):

| Commit | What |
|---|---|
| `8cd3d63` | `feat(deob): FULL decompiled client compiles to 0 errors (M0)` |
| `8f3d764` | `chore(net): refresh server RSA key, add account-register tool` |
| `5b4c41d` | `deob(rsc-client): checkpoint — Boot entrypoint, Mudclient/World/Scene/net work` |
| `43b379b` | `fix(deob): runtime fixes from M1/M2 bring-up — qsort recursion + live rasterizer` |
| `080b7c1` | `feat(client): auto-confirm appearance on login + make NPCs/players/items pickable` |
| `e64d617` | `feat(deob): clean world frame + live login — decompiled client reaches M3+M4` |
| `a12dbdb` | `feat(deob): stable in-game session — post-login dispatch + 49 split-field consolidation` |
| `f767f6a` | `feat(deob): stable in-game session — post-login dispatch + 49 split-field consolidation` |
| `cdbc6b2` | `feat(deob): live in-game 3D world renders — FULLY FUNCTIONING decompiled client` |

This workflow's hardening pass (uncommitted at time of writing; the parent handles
commit + push) adds the two split-field fixes above plus the `RSC_AUTO_WALK` /
`RSC_AUTO_TABS` bring-up hooks — net diff is **`Mudclient.java` only, +63/−14**;
`Surface.java` was reverted byte-identical. Only `reference/rsc-client/` was touched.

---

## WHAT WORKS

Boot · autologin · auto-appearance · stable multi-minute live session · live 3D world
render (93–94% non-black viewport) with correct walls/floor/doorway/terrain/lighting ·
camera follow · **player movement** (route + walk packets + op-191 position updates) ·
region-origin tracking · all wired UI tabs render crash-free · server "Welcome"/chat
overlays render with real account data.

## WHAT'S NEXT (all deferred follow-ups — not crashes)

1. **Full in-game playability polish.** Wire the disabled **stats/skills tab (`qc==3`)**
   (needs an Assemble pass to recover its drawer), and door-aware navigation so auto-walk
   can cross region boundaries (the spawn is a walled room, so most walk legs correctly
   return `sent=false` — that is correct pathfinding, not a bug).
2. **Render-fidelity follow-up.** The rectangular texture/lighting discontinuity on the
   right of the viewport (a lighter overlaid rectangle with a sharp floor seam) — a
   shading fidelity quirk in the scenery/lighting tile path, not a crash.
3. **`Mudclient` → helper-class refactor.** The 11.6k-line `Mudclient.java` can now be
   split into helper classes — **now safe because the compiler enforces correctness**
   (you couldn't safely refactor a non-compiling tree).
4. **Upstream OpenRSC render-fidelity rebase.** Forward-port the genuine orsc render gaps
   per [RENDER_RECONCILIATION.md](RENDER_RECONCILIATION.md) (the under-roof cull, the
   id-74 windmill-sail lift, and the 48001..59999 diagonal scenery/door band), and adopt
   orsc where it is more byte-faithful (multi-story roof anchors / plane-cap).

---

## Build / verification state

Tree compiles **0 errors / 71 classes** under JDK 17
(`javac -d /tmp/deob-run -encoding UTF-8 @/tmp/srcs.txt`; the two `java.applet` removal
warnings are expected). All temporary diagnostics were removed; no git commit was made by
this workflow (the parent handles commit + push). Only `reference/rsc-client/` was
touched.
